Home/Course/Exercises

Exercises

This section contains practice problems and coding exercises to reinforce learning.

Beginner Exercises

#
^

A set of exercises designed for beginners to practice basic programming concepts.

Hello World

#
^

Write a program that prints 'Hello, World!' to the console.

Solution
CPP
#include <iostream>

int main() {
    std::cout << "Hello, World!/n";
    return 0;
}
GO
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	fmt.Println("Hello, World!")
}
PYTHON
print("Hello, World!")
SCALA2
object Main extends App {
  println("Hello, World!")
}
SCALA3
@main def hello(): Unit =
  println("Hello, World!")
TYPESCRIPT
console.log("Hello, World!");

Sum of Numbers

#
^

Write a program that calculates the sum of the first n natural numbers.

Solution
CPP
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

long long sum_n(long long n) {
    return n * (n + 1) / 2;
}

int main() {
    long long n;
    // Example: input a number, prints the sum of 1..n
    if (!(cin >> n)) n = 10;
    cout << sum_n(n) << "/n";
    return 0;
}
GO
package main

import (
	"fmt"
)

func sumN(n int64) int64 {
	return n * (n + 1) / 2
}

func main() {
	var n int64
	if _, err := fmt.Scan(&n); err != nil {
		n = 10
	}
	fmt.Println(sumN(n))
}
PYTHON
def sum_n(n: int) -> int:
    return n * (n + 1) // 2

if __name__ == '__main__':
    try:
        n = int(input().strip())
    except Exception:
        n = 10
    print(sum_n(n))
SCALA2
object SumOfNumbers {
  def sumN(n: Int): Int = n * (n + 1) / 2
  def main(args: Array[String]): Unit = {
    val n = if (args.nonEmpty) args(0).toInt else 10
    println(sumN(n))
  }
}
SCALA3
@main def sumOfNumbers(n: Int = 10): Unit =
  println(n * (n + 1) / 2)
TYPESCRIPT
function sumN(n: number): number {
  return (n * (n + 1)) / 2;
}

const fs = require('fs');
const input = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8').trim().split(//s+/);
const n = input.length ? Number(input[0]) : 10;
console.log(sumN(n));

Factorial Calculation

#
^

Write a program that calculates the factorial of a given number.

Solution
CPP
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

long long factorial(int n) {
    long long r = 1;
    for (int i = 2; i <= n; ++i) r *= i;
    return r;
}

int main() {
    int n;
    if (!(cin >> n)) n = 5;
    cout << factorial(n) << "/n";
    return 0;
}
GO
package main

import (
	"fmt"
)

func factorial(n int64) int64 {
	var r int64 = 1
	for i := int64(2); i <= n; i++ {
		r *= i
	}
	return r
}

func main() {
	var n int64
	if _, err := fmt.Scan(&n); err != nil {
		n = 5
	}
	fmt.Println(factorial(n))
}
PYTHON
def factorial(n: int) -> int:
    r = 1
    for i in range(2, n+1):
        r *= i
    return r

if __name__ == '__main__':
    try:
        n = int(input().strip())
    except Exception:
        n = 5
    print(factorial(n))
SCALA2
object Factorial {
  def fact(n: Int): BigInt = if (n <= 1) 1 else (2 to n).map(BigInt(_)).product
  def main(args: Array[String]): Unit = {
    val n = if (args.nonEmpty) args(0).toInt else 5
    println(fact(n))
  }
}
SCALA3
object Factorial:
  def fact(n: Int): BigInt = if n <= 1 then 1 else (2 to n).map(BigInt(_)).product
  @main def run(n: Int = 5) = println(fact(n))
TYPESCRIPT
function factorial(n: number): number {
  let r = 1;
  for (let i = 2; i <= n; i++) r *= i;
  return r;
}

const fs = require('fs');
const input = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8').trim().split(//s+/);
const n = input.length ? Number(input[0]) : 5;
console.log(factorial(n));

Prime Number Check

#
^

Write a program that checks if a given number is prime.

Solution
CPP
#include <iostream>
#include <cmath>
using namespace std;

bool is_prime(long long n) {
    if (n < 2) return false;
    if (n % 2 == 0) return n == 2;
    for (long long i = 3; i * i <= n; i += 2) if (n % i == 0) return false;
    return true;
}

int main() {
    long long n;
    if (!(cin >> n)) n = 97;
    cout << (is_prime(n) ? "true" : "false") << "/n";
}
GO
package main

import (
	"fmt"
)

func isPrime(n int64) bool {
	if n < 2 {
		return false
	}
	if n%2 == 0 {
		return n == 2
	}
	for i := int64(3); i*i <= n; i += 2 {
		if n%i == 0 {
			return false
		}
	}
	return true
}

func main() {
	var n int64
	if _, err := fmt.Scan(&n); err != nil {
		n = 97
	}
	fmt.Println(isPrime(n))
}
PYTHON
import math

def is_prime(n: int) -> bool:
    if n < 2:
        return False
    if n % 2 == 0:
        return n == 2
    i = 3
    while i * i <= n:
        if n % i == 0:
            return False
        i += 2
    return True

if __name__ == '__main__':
    try:
        n = int(input().strip())
    except Exception:
        n = 97
    print(str(is_prime(n)).lower())
SCALA2
object PrimeCheck {
  def isPrime(n: Int): Boolean = {
    if (n < 2) return false
    if (n % 2 == 0) return n == 2
    var i = 3
    while (i * i <= n) {
      if (n % i == 0) return false
      i += 2
    }
    true
  }
  def main(args: Array[String]): Unit = {
    val n = if (args.nonEmpty) args(0).toInt else 97
    println(isPrime(n))
  }
}
SCALA3
object PrimeCheck:
  def isPrime(n: Int): Boolean =
    if n < 2 then false
    else if n % 2 == 0 then n == 2
    else (3 to math.sqrt(n).toInt by 2).forall(n % _ != 0)
  @main def run(n: Int = 97) = println(isPrime(n))
TYPESCRIPT
function isPrime(n: number): boolean {
  if (n < 2) return false;
  if (n % 2 === 0) return n === 2;
  for (let i = 3; i * i <= n; i += 2) if (n % i === 0) return false;
  return true;
}

const fs = require('fs');
const input = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8').trim().split(//s+/);
const n = input.length ? Number(input[0]) : 97;
console.log(isPrime(n));

Maximum of an Array

#
^

Write a program that finds the maximum element in an array.

Solution
CPP
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <limits>
using namespace std;

int main() {
    int n;
    if (!(cin >> n)) n = 5;
    vector<long long> a(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (!(cin >> a[i])) a[i] = i;
    }
    long long mx = numeric_limits<long long>::min();
    for (auto v : a) mx = max(mx, v);
    cout << mx << "/n";
    return 0;
}
GO
package main

import (
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	var n int
	if _, err := fmt.Scan(&n); err != nil {
		n = 5
	}
	arr := make([]int64, n)
	for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
		if _, err := fmt.Scan(&arr[i]); err != nil {
			arr[i] = int64(i)
		}
	}
	var mx int64 = arr[0]
	for _, v := range arr {
		if v > mx {
			mx = v
		}
	}
	fmt.Println(mx)
}
PYTHON
import sys

def max_of_array(arr):
    return max(arr) if arr else None

if __name__ == '__main__':
    try:
        parts = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
        if parts:
            n = int(parts[0]); vals = list(map(int, parts[1:1+n]))
        else:
            n = 5; vals = [1,2,3,4,5]
    except Exception:
        n = 5; vals = [1,2,3,4,5]
    print(max_of_array(vals))
SCALA2
object MaxOfArray {
  def main(args: Array[String]): Unit = {
    val arr = if (args.nonEmpty) args.map(_.toInt) else Array(1,2,3,4,5)
    println(arr.max)
  }
}
SCALA3
@main def maxOfArray(args: String*): Unit =
  val arr = if args.nonEmpty then args.map(_.toInt).toArray else Array(1,2,3,4,5)
  println(arr.max)
TYPESCRIPT
const fs = require('fs');
const input = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8').trim().split(//s+/);
let idx = 0;
let n = input.length ? Number(input[idx++]) : 5;
const arr = [];
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) arr.push(input.length > idx ? Number(input[idx++]) : i+1);
console.log(Math.max(...arr));

Even-Odd Check

#
^

Determine if a number is even or odd.

Solution
CPP
#include <iostream>

int main() {
    int n;
    std::cout << "Enter a number: ";
    std::cin >> n;
    
    if (n % 2 == 0)
        std::cout << n << " is even/n";
    else
        std::cout << n << " is odd/n";
    return 0;
}
GO
package main

import (
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	var n int
	fmt.Print("Enter a number: ")
	fmt.Scan(&n)

	if n%2 == 0 {
		fmt.Printf("%d is even/n", n)
	} else {
		fmt.Printf("%d is odd/n", n)
	}
}
PYTHON
n = int(input("Enter a number: "))
if n % 2 == 0:
    print(f"{n} is even")
else:
    print(f"{n} is odd")
SCALA2
val n = scala.io.StdIn.readInt()
if (n % 2 == 0) println(s"$n is even")
else println(s"$n is odd")
SCALA3
val n = scala.io.StdIn.readInt()
if n % 2 == 0 then println(s"$n is even")
else println(s"$n is odd")
TYPESCRIPT
import * as readline from "readline";
const rl = readline.createInterface({ input: process.stdin, output: process.stdout });
rl.question("Enter a number: ", (input) => {
  const n = parseInt(input);
  console.log(n % 2 === 0 ? `${n} is even` : `${n} is odd`);
  rl.close();
});
import * as readline from "readline";

const rl = readline.createInterface({ input: process.stdin, output: process.stdout });
rl.question("Enter a number: ", (input) => {
  const n = parseInt(input);
  console.log(n % 2 === 0 ? `${n} is even` : `${n} is odd`);
  rl.close();
});

FizzBuzz

#
^

Print numbers from 1 to n. For multiples of 3, print 'Fizz' instead of the number, and for multiples of 5, print 'Buzz'. For numbers which are multiples of both 3 and 5, print 'FizzBuzz'.

Solution
CPP
#include <iostream>

int main() {
    for (int i = 1; i <= 100; ++i) {
        if (i % 15 == 0) std::cout << "FizzBuzz/n";
        else if (i % 3 == 0) std::cout << "Fizz/n";
        else if (i % 5 == 0) std::cout << "Buzz/n";
        else std::cout << i << "/n";
    }
    return 0;
}
GO
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	for i := 1; i <= 100; i++ {
		switch {
		case i%15 == 0:
			fmt.Println("FizzBuzz")
		case i%3 == 0:
			fmt.Println("Fizz")
		case i%5 == 0:
			fmt.Println("Buzz")
		default:
			fmt.Println(i)
		}
	}
}
PYTHON
for i in range(1, 101):
    if i % 15 == 0: print("FizzBuzz")
    elif i % 3 == 0: print("Fizz")
    elif i % 5 == 0: print("Buzz")
    else: print(i)
SCALA2
(1 to 100).foreach { i =>
  if (i % 15 == 0) println("FizzBuzz")
  else if (i % 3 == 0) println("Fizz")
  else if (i % 5 == 0) println("Buzz")
  else println(i)
}
SCALA3
(1 to 100).foreach { i =>
  if i % 15 == 0 then println("FizzBuzz")
  else if i % 3 == 0 then println("Fizz")
  else if i % 5 == 0 then println("Buzz")
  else println(i)
}
TYPESCRIPT
for (let i = 1; i <= 100; i++) {
  if (i % 15 === 0) console.log("FizzBuzz");
  else if (i % 3 === 0) console.log("Fizz");
  else if (i % 5 === 0) console.log("Buzz");
  else console.log(i);
}
for (let i = 1; i <= 100; i++) {
  if (i % 15 === 0) console.log("FizzBuzz");
  else if (i % 3 === 0) console.log("Fizz");
  else if (i % 5 === 0) console.log("Buzz");
  else console.log(i);
}

Intermediate Exercises

#
^

A set of exercises designed for intermediate learners to practice more complex programming concepts.

Reverse a String

#
^

Write a program that reverses a given string.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/reverse_string" missing

Binary Search

#
^

Implement the binary search algorithm to find the index of a target value in a sorted array.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/binary_search" missing

Merge Sort

#
^

Implement the merge sort algorithm to sort an array.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/merge_sort" missing

Anagram Check

#
^

Write a program that checks if two strings are anagrams of each other.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/anagram_check" missing

Stack Implementation

#
^

Implement a stack with basic operations such as push, pop, and peek.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/stack_implementation" missing

Queue Implementation

#
^

Implement a queue with basic operations such as enqueue, dequeue, and peek.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/queue_implementation" missing

Binary Tree Traversal

#
^

Implement in-order, pre-order, and post-order traversal of a binary tree.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/binary_tree_traversal" missing

Divide and Conquer: Quick Sort

#
^

Implement the quick sort algorithm using the divide and conquer approach.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/quick_sort" missing

Greedy Algorithm: Huffman Coding

#
^

Implement Huffman coding for data compression using a greedy algorithm.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/huffman_coding" missing

Backtracking: Sudoku Solver

#
^

Solve a Sudoku puzzle using backtracking.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/sudoku_solver" missing

Dynamic Programming: Longest Common Subsequence

#
^

Find the longest common subsequence of two strings using dynamic programming.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/longest_common_subsequence" missing

Divide and Conquer: Closest Pair of Points

#
^

Find the closest pair of points in a 2D plane using a divide and conquer algorithm.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/closest_pair_of_points" missing

Greedy Algorithm: Kruskal's Algorithm

#
^

Implement Kruskal's algorithm to find the minimum spanning tree of a graph.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/kruskal_algorithm" missing

Backtracking: Permutations of a String

#
^

Generate all permutations of a given string using backtracking.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/string_permutations" missing

Dynamic Programming: Coin Change Problem

#
^

Determine the number of ways to make change for a given amount using a set of coin denominations.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/coin_change_problem" missing

Divide and Conquer: Matrix Multiplication

#
^

Implement matrix multiplication using the divide and conquer approach.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/matrix_multiply" missing

Backtracking: Combination Sum

#
^

Find all unique combinations of numbers that sum up to a target value using backtracking.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/combination_sum" missing

Dynamic Programming: Edit Distance

#
^

Calculate the edit distance (Levenshtein distance) between two strings using dynamic programming.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/edit_distance" missing

Divide and Conquer: Count Inversions in an Array

#
^

Count the number of inversions in an array using a divide and conquer algorithm.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/count_inversions" missing

Greedy Algorithm: Job Scheduling Problem

#
^

Schedule jobs to maximize profit using a greedy algorithm.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/job_scheduling_problem" missing

Backtracking: Subset Sum Problem

#
^

Determine if there is a subset of a given set of numbers that sums up to a target value using backtracking.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/subset_sum_problem" missing

Dynamic Programming: Longest Increasing Subsequence

#
^

Find the length of the longest increasing subsequence in an array using dynamic programming.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/longest_increasing_subsequence" missing

Divide and Conquer: Count Occurrences in a Sorted Array

#
^

Count the number of occurrences of a target value in a sorted array using a divide and conquer algorithm.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/count_occurrences" missing

Greedy Algorithm: Fractional Knapsack Problem

#
^

Solve the fractional knapsack problem using a greedy algorithm.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/fractional_knapsack_problem" missing

Dynamic Programming: Palindrome Partitioning

#
^

Partition a string into all possible palindrome partitions using dynamic programming.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/palindrome_partitioning" missing

Divide and Conquer: Majority Element

#
^

Find the majority element in an array using a divide and conquer algorithm.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/majority_element" missing

Greedy Algorithm: Huffman Coding

#
^

Implement Huffman coding for data compression using a greedy algorithm.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/huffman_coding" missing

Backtracking: Word Search

#
^

Determine if a given word exists in a 2D grid of characters using backtracking.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/word_search" missing

Linked List Implementation

#
^

Implement a singly linked list with basic operations such as insertion, deletion, and traversal.

Snippet Error: "exercises/intermediate/linked_list" missing

Array Reverse

#
^

Reverse an array in place.

Solution
CPP
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>

int main() {
    std::vector<int> arr = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5};
    std::reverse(arr.begin(), arr.end());
    
    for (int x : arr) {
        std::cout << x << " ";
    }
    std::cout << "/n";
    return 0;
}
GO
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	arr := []int{1, 2, 3, 4, 5}
	for i, j := 0, len(arr)-1; i < j; i, j = i+1, j-1 {
		arr[i], arr[j] = arr[j], arr[i]
	}
	fmt.Println(arr)
}
PYTHON
arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
arr.reverse()  # or arr = arr[::-1]
print(arr)
SCALA2
val arr = List(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
val reversed = arr.reverse
println(reversed)
SCALA3
val arr = List(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
val reversed = arr.reverse
println(reversed)
TYPESCRIPT
const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
arr.reverse();
console.log(arr);
const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
arr.reverse();
console.log(arr);

Fibonacci Sequence

#
^

Generate the Fibonacci sequence up to a given number.

Solution
CPP
#include <iostream>

int main() {
    int n;
    std::cout << "Enter number of terms: ";
    std::cin >> n;
    
    long long a = 0, b = 1;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        std::cout << a << " ";
        long long temp = a + b;
        a = b;
        b = temp;
    }
    std::cout << "/n";
    return 0;
}
GO
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	var n int
	fmt.Print("Enter number of terms: ")
	fmt.Scan(&n)
	a, b := 0, 1
	for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
		fmt.Print(a, " ")
		a, b = b, a+b
	}
	fmt.Println()
}
PYTHON
n = int(input("Enter number of terms: "))
a, b = 0, 1
for _ in range(n):
    print(a, end=" ")
    a, b = b, a + b
print()
SCALA2
val n = scala.io.StdIn.readInt()
var a = 0L
var b = 1L
for (_ <- 0 until n) {
  print(a + " ")
  val temp = a + b
  a = b
  b = temp
}
println()
SCALA3
val n = scala.io.StdIn.readInt()
var a = 0L
var b = 1L
for _ <- 0 until n do
  print(a + " ")
  val temp = a + b
  a = b
  b = temp
println()
TYPESCRIPT
const n = parseInt(process.argv[2] || "10");
let a = 0, b = 1;
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
  process.stdout.write(a + " ");
  [a, b] = [b, a + b];
}
console.log();
const n = parseInt(process.argv[2] || "10");
let a = 0, b = 1;
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
  process.stdout.write(a + " ");
  [a, b] = [b, a + b];
}
console.log();

Palindrome Check

#
^

Check if a string is a palindrome.

Solution
CPP
#include <algorithm>
#include <cctype>
#include <iostream>
#include <string>

int main() {
    std::string s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama";
    std::string cleaned;
    for (char c : s) {
        if (std::isalnum(static_cast<unsigned char>(c))) {
            cleaned += std::tolower(static_cast<unsigned char>(c));
        }
    }
    
    bool isPalin = std::string(cleaned.rbegin(), cleaned.rend()) == cleaned;
    std::cout << (isPalin ? "Palindrome" : "Not a palindrome") << "/n";
    return 0;
}
GO
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"regexp"
	"strings"
)

func main() {
	s := "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
	re := regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9]`)
	cleaned := strings.ToLower(re.ReplaceAllString(s, ""))

	rev := ""
	for _, c := range cleaned {
		rev = string(c) + rev
	}

	if cleaned == rev {
		fmt.Println("Palindrome")
	} else {
		fmt.Println("Not a palindrome")
	}
}
PYTHON
s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
cleaned = "".join(c.lower() for c in s if c.isalnum())
is_palin = cleaned == cleaned[::-1]
print("Palindrome" if is_palin else "Not a palindrome")
SCALA2
val s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
val cleaned = s.filter(_.isLetterOrDigit).toLowerCase
val isPalin = cleaned == cleaned.reverse
println(if (isPalin) "Palindrome" else "Not a palindrome")
SCALA3
val s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
val cleaned = s.filter(_.isLetterOrDigit).toLowerCase
val isPalin = cleaned == cleaned.reverse
println(if isPalin then "Palindrome" else "Not a palindrome")
TYPESCRIPT
const s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama";
const cleaned = s.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/g, "").toLowerCase();
const isPalin = cleaned === cleaned.split("").reverse().join("");
console.log(isPalin ? "Palindrome" : "Not a palindrome");
function isPalindrome(s: string): boolean {
  const cleaned = s.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, "");
  return cleaned === cleaned.split("").reverse().join("");
}

console.log(isPalindrome("A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"));

Advanced Exercises

#
^

Graph Traversal: Depth-First Search and Breadth-First Search

#
^

Implement depth-first search (DFS) and breadth-first search (BFS) algorithms for graph traversal.

Solution
CPP
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;
int main(){
    vector<vector<int>> g = {{1,2},{0,3},{0,3},{1,2}}; // simple graph
    // BFS
    vector<int> vis(4); queue<int>q; q.push(0); vis[0]=1;
    cout<<"BFS:";
    while(!q.empty()){int u=q.front();q.pop(); cout<<" "<<u; for(int v:g[u]) if(!vis[v]){vis[v]=1; q.push(v);} }
    cout<<"/n";
    // DFS
    vector<int> seen(4); function<void(int)> dfs=[&](int u){ seen[u]=1; cout<<u<<" "; for(int v:g[u]) if(!seen[v]) dfs(v); };
    cout<<"DFS: "; dfs(0); cout<<"/n"; }
GO
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	g := [][]int{{1, 2}, {0, 3}, {0, 3}, {1, 2}}
	// BFS
	q := []int{0}
	seen := map[int]bool{0: true}
	fmt.Print("BFS:")
	for len(q) > 0 {
		u := q[0]
		q = q[1:]
		fmt.Print(" ", u)
		for _, v := range g[u] {
			if !seen[v] {
				seen[v] = true
				q = append(q, v)
			}
		}
	}
	fmt.Println()
	// DFS
	fmt.Print("DFS: ")
	seen = map[int]bool{}
	var dfs func(int)
	dfs = func(u int) {
		seen[u] = true
		fmt.Print(u, " ")
		for _, v := range g[u] {
			if !seen[v] {
				dfs(v)
			}
		}
	}
	dfs(0)
	fmt.Println()
}
PYTHON
from collections import deque

g = {0:[1,2],1:[0,3],2:[0,3],3:[1,2]}
print('BFS:', end=' ')
q=deque([0]); seen={0}
while q:
    u=q.popleft(); print(u, end=' ')
    for v in g[u]:
        if v not in seen: seen.add(v); q.append(v)
print('/nDFS:', end=' ')
seen=set()
import sys
sys.setrecursionlimit(10000)
def dfs(u):
    seen.add(u); print(u, end=' ')
    for v in g[u]:
        if v not in seen: dfs(v)

dfs(0)
SCALA2
object GraphTraversal extends App{
  val g = Array(Array(1,2),Array(0,3),Array(0,3),Array(1,2))
  println("BFS:")
  import scala.collection.mutable.Queue
  val q = Queue(0); val seen = scala.collection.mutable.Set(0)
  while(q.nonEmpty){ val u=q.dequeue(); print(u+" "); for(v<-g(u)) if(!seen(v)){ seen += v; q.enqueue(v) } }
  println();
  print("DFS: "); val s = scala.collection.mutable.Set[Int]()
  def dfs(u:Int):Unit={ s += u; print(u+" "); for(v<-g(u)) if(!s(v)) dfs(v) }
  dfs(0); println();
}
SCALA3
@main def runGraph() =
  val g = Array(Array(1,2),Array(0,3),Array(0,3),Array(1,2))
  print("BFS:")
  import scala.collection.mutable.Queue
  val q = Queue(0); val seen = scala.collection.mutable.Set(0)
  while q.nonEmpty do val u = q.dequeue(); print(s" $u"); for v <- g(u) do if !seen(v) then seen += v; q.enqueue(v)
  println()
  print("DFS:"); val s = scala.collection.mutable.Set[Int]()
  def dfs(u:Int):Unit = { s += u; print(s" $u"); for v <- g(u) do if !s(v) then dfs(v) }
  dfs(0); println()
TYPESCRIPT
const g: number[][] = [[1,2],[0,3],[0,3],[1,2]]
console.log('BFS:');
{
  const q = [0]; const seen = new Set([0]);
  while(q.length){ const u = q.shift()!; process.stdout.write(' '+u); for(const v of g[u]) if(!seen.has(v)){ seen.add(v); q.push(v); } }
  console.log();
}
console.log('DFS:');
{ const seen = new Set<number>(); function dfs(u:number){ seen.add(u); process.stdout.write(' '+u); for(const v of g[u]) if(!seen.has(v)) dfs(v); } dfs(0); console.log(); }
export {}

Dijkstra's Algorithm for Shortest Path

#
^

Implement Dijkstra's algorithm to find the shortest path in a weighted graph.

Solution
CPP
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;
int main(){
    int n=5; vector<vector<pair<int,int>>> g(n);
    g[0].push_back({1,10}); g[0].push_back({2,3}); g[2].push_back({1,1}); g[1].push_back({3,2}); g[2].push_back({3,8});
    const long long INF=1e18; vector<long long> d(n,INF); d[0]=0; priority_queue<pair<long long,int>,vector<pair<long long,int>>,greater<pair<long long,int>>>pq; pq.push({0,0});
    while(!pq.empty()){ auto [dist,u]=pq.top(); pq.pop(); if(dist!=d[u]) continue; for(auto [v,w]:g[u]) if(d[v]>d[u]+w){ d[v]=d[u]+w; pq.push({d[v],v}); } }
    for(int i=0;i<n;i++) cout<<"dist["<<i<<"]="<<d[i]<<"/n";
}
GO
package main

import (
	"container/heap"
	"fmt"
)

type Item struct {
	v int
	d int
}
type PQ []Item

func (p PQ) Len() int            { return len(p) }
func (p PQ) Less(i, j int) bool  { return p[i].d < p[j].d }
func (p PQ) Swap(i, j int)       { p[i], p[j] = p[j], p[i] }
func (p *PQ) Push(x interface{}) { *p = append(*p, x.(Item)) }
func (p *PQ) Pop() interface{}   { old := *p; n := len(old); x := old[n-1]; *p = old[:n-1]; return x }

func dijkstra(src int, g map[int][][]int) map[int]int {
	INF := 1 << 30
	dist := map[int]int{}
	for k := range g {
		dist[k] = INF
	}
	dist[src] = 0
	pq := &PQ{{v: src, d: 0}}
	heap.Init(pq)
	for pq.Len() > 0 {
		it := heap.Pop(pq).(Item)
		if it.d != dist[it.v] {
			continue
		}
		for _, e := range g[it.v] {
			v := e[0]
			w := e[1]
			if dist[v] > it.d+w {
				dist[v] = it.d + w
				heap.Push(pq, Item{v: v, d: dist[v]})
			}
		}
	}
	return dist
}
func main() {
	g := map[int][][]int{0: {{1, 10}, {2, 3}}, 1: {{3, 2}}, 2: {{1, 1}, {3, 8}}, 3: {}}
	fmt.Println(dijkstra(0, g))
}
PYTHON
import heapq

g = {0:[(1,10),(2,3)], 1:[(3,2)], 2:[(1,1),(3,8)], 3:[]}
INF=10**9

def dijkstra(src):
    dist = {v:INF for v in g}
    dist[src]=0; pq=[(0,src)]
    while pq:
        d,u = heapq.heappop(pq)
        if d!=dist[u]: continue
        for v,w in g[u]:
            if dist[v]>d+w:
                dist[v]=d+w; heapq.heappush(pq,(dist[v],v))
    return dist

print(dijkstra(0))
SCALA2
object Dijkstra extends App{
  val g = Map(0->List((1,10),(2,3)),1->List((3,2)),2->List((1,1),(3,8)),3->List())
  import scala.collection.mutable.PriorityQueue
  implicit val ord = Ordering.by[(Int,Int),Int](-_._2)
  def dijkstra(src:Int) ={
    val dist = scala.collection.mutable.Map(g.keys.toSeq.map(k=>k->Int.MaxValue):_*); dist(src)=0
    val pq = PriorityQueue((src,0))
    while(pq.nonEmpty){ val (u,du)=pq.dequeue(); if(du==dist(u)) for((v,w)<-g(u)) if(dist(v)>du+w){ dist(v)=du+w; pq.enqueue((v,dist(v))) } }
    dist
  }
  println(dijkstra(0))
}
SCALA3
@main def runDijkstra() =
  val g = Map(0->List((1,10),(2,3)),1->List((3,2)),2->List((1,1),(3,8)),3->List())
  import scala.collection.mutable.PriorityQueue
  given ord: Ordering[(Int,Int)] = Ordering.by(-_._2)
  def dijkstra(src:Int) =
    val dist = scala.collection.mutable.Map(g.keys.toSeq.map(k=>k->Int.MaxValue):_*)
    dist(src)=0
    val pq = PriorityQueue((src,0))
    while pq.nonEmpty do
      val (u,du) = pq.dequeue()
      if du==dist(u) then for ( (v,w) <- g(u) ) if dist(v) > du + w then dist(v)=du+w; pq.enqueue((v,dist(v)))
    dist
  println(dijkstra(0))
TYPESCRIPT
type Edge = [number,number]
const g: Record<number,Edge[]> = {0:[[1,10],[2,3]],1:[[3,2]],2:[[1,1],[3,8]],3:[]}
const INF = 1e9
function dijkstra(src:number){ const dist = new Map<number,number>(); for(const k in g) dist.set(Number(k), INF); dist.set(src,0);
  const pq: [number,number][] = [[0,src]]
  while(pq.length){ pq.sort((a,b)=>a[0]-b[0]); const [d,u]=pq.shift()!; if(d!==dist.get(u)) continue; for(const [v,w] of g[u]) if((dist.get(v)??INF) > d + w){ dist.set(v,d+w); pq.push([d+w,v]); } }
  return Object.fromEntries(dist)
}
console.log(dijkstra(0))
export {}

Dynamic Programming: Knapsack Problem

#
^

Solve the 0/1 knapsack problem using dynamic programming.

Solution
CPP
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;int main(){
    vector<int> wt={3,4,5}, val={30,50,60}; int W=8, n=wt.size();
    vector<vector<int>> dp(n+1, vector<int>(W+1));
    for(int i=1;i<=n;i++) for(int w=0;w<=W;w++) dp[i][w]=dp[i-1][w];
    for(int i=1;i<=n;i++) for(int w=wt[i-1];w<=W;w++) dp[i][w]=max(dp[i][w], dp[i-1][w-wt[i-1]]+val[i-1]);
    cout<<dp[n][W]<<"/n";
}
GO
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	wt := []int{3, 4, 5}
	val := []int{30, 50, 60}
	W := 8
	n := len(wt)
	dp := make([][]int, n+1)
	for i := 0; i <= n; i++ {
		dp[i] = make([]int, W+1)
	}
	for i := 1; i <= n; i++ {
		for w := 0; w <= W; w++ {
			dp[i][w] = dp[i-1][w]
			if w >= wt[i-1] {
				if cand := dp[i-1][w-wt[i-1]] + val[i-1]; cand > dp[i][w] {
					dp[i][w] = cand
				}
			}
		}
	}
	fmt.Println(dp[n][W])
}
PYTHON
wt=[3,4,5]
val=[30,50,60]
W=8
n=len(wt)
dp=[[0]*(W+1) for _ in range(n+1)]
for i in range(1,n+1):
    for w in range(W+1):
        dp[i][w]=dp[i-1][w]
        if w>=wt[i-1]: dp[i][w]=max(dp[i][w], dp[i-1][w-wt[i-1]]+val[i-1])
print(dp[n][W])
SCALA2
object Knapsack extends App{
  val wt = Array(3,4,5); val v = Array(30,50,60); val W=8; val n=wt.length
  val dp = Array.fill(n+1, W+1)(0)
  for(i<-1+1 to n) for(w<-0 to W){ dp(i)(w)=dp(i-1)(w); if(w>=wt(i-1)) dp(i)(w)=math.max(dp(i)(w), dp(i-1)(w-wt(i-1))+v(i-1)) }
  println(dp(n)(W))
}
SCALA3
@main def runKnapsack() =
  val wt = Array(3,4,5); val v = Array(30,50,60); val W=8; val n=wt.length
  val dp = Array.fill(n+1, W+1)(0)
  for i <- 1 to n do for w <- 0 to W do dp(i)(w) = math.max(dp(i)(w), if w>=wt(i-1) then dp(i-1)(w-wt(i-1))+v(i-1) else dp(i-1)(w))
  println(dp(n)(W))
TYPESCRIPT
const wt = [3,4,5], val=[30,50,60], W=8
const n=wt.length
const dp: number[][] = Array.from({length:n+1},()=>Array(W+1).fill(0))
for(let i=1;i<=n;i++) for(let w=0; w<=W; w++){ dp[i][w]=dp[i-1][w]; if(w>=wt[i-1]) dp[i][w]=Math.max(dp[i][w], dp[i-1][w-wt[i-1]]+val[i-1]) }
console.log(dp[n][W])
export {}

Backtracking: N-Queens Problem

#
^

Solve the N-Queens problem using backtracking.

Solution
CPP
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;
int N=8; vector<int> col;
bool ok(int r,int c){ for(int i=0;i<r;i++) if(col[i]==c||abs(col[i]-c)==r-i) return false; return true; }
void solve(int r){ if(r==N){ for(int i=0;i<N;i++) cout<<col[i]<<" "; cout<<"/n"; exit(0);} for(int c=0;c<N;c++) if(ok(r,c)){ col[r]=c; solve(r+1); }}
int main(){ col.assign(N,0); solve(0); }
GO
package main
import "fmt"
var N=8
var cols = make([]int, N)
func ok(r,c int) bool{ for i:=0;i<r;i++{ if cols[i]==c || abs(cols[i]-c)==r-i { return false } } return true }
func abs(x int) int{ if x<0 { return -x }; return x }
func solve(r int) bool{ if r==N { fmt.Println(cols); return true }; for c:=0;c<N;c++{ if ok(r,c){ cols[r]=c; if solve(r+1){ return true } } } return false }
func main(){ solve(0) }
PYTHON
N=8
cols=[0]*N

def ok(r,c):
    for i in range(r):
        if cols[i]==c or abs(cols[i]-c)==r-i: return False
    return True

def solve(r):
    if r==N:
        print(cols); return True
    for c in range(N):
        if ok(r,c):
            cols[r]=c
            if solve(r+1): return True
    return False

if __name__=='__main__': solve(0)
SCALA2
object NQueens extends App{
  val N=8; val cols = Array.fill(N)(0)
  def ok(r:Int,c:Int)= (0 until r).forall(i=> cols(i)!=c && math.abs(cols(i)-c)!=r-i)
  def solve(r:Int):Boolean = if(r==N){ println(cols.mkString(",")); true } else { for(c<-0 until N) if(ok(r,c)){ cols(r)=c; if(solve(r+1)) return true }; false }
  solve(0)
}
SCALA3
@main def runNQueens() =
  val N = 8
  val cols = Array.fill(N)(0)
  def ok(r:Int,c:Int) = (0 until r).forall(i => cols(i)!=c && math.abs(cols(i)-c)!=r-i)
  def solve(r:Int):Boolean =
    if r==N then println(cols.mkString(",")); true
    else
      for c <- 0 until N do
        if ok(r,c) then cols(r)=c; if solve(r+1) then return true
      false
  solve(0)
TYPESCRIPT
const N = 8
const cols = new Array<number>(N).fill(0)
function ok(r:number,c:number){ for(let i=0;i<r;i++) if(cols[i]===c || Math.abs(cols[i]-c)===r-i) return false; return true }
function solve(r=0): boolean{ if(r===N){ console.log(cols); return true } for(let c=0;c<N;c++){ if(ok(r,c)){ cols[r]=c; if(solve(r+1)) return true } } return false }
solve(); export {}

Greedy Algorithm: Activity Selection Problem

#
^

Solve the activity selection problem using a greedy algorithm.

Solution
CPP
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;int main(){ vector<pair<int,int>> acts={{1,4},{3,5},{0,6},{5,7},{8,9},{5,9}}; sort(acts.begin(), acts.end(), [](auto &a, auto &b){ return a.second < b.second; }); int last= -1; cout<<"Selected:"; for(auto &p:acts) if(p.first>last){ cout<<" ("<<p.first<<","<<p.second<<")"; last=p.second; } cout<<"/n"; }
GO
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	acts := [][2]int{{1, 4}, {3, 5}, {0, 6}, {5, 7}, {8, 9}, {5, 9}}
	// sort by end (simple bubble for brevity)
	for i := 0; i < len(acts); i++ {
		for j := i + 1; j < len(acts); j++ {
			if acts[j][1] < acts[i][1] {
				acts[i], acts[j] = acts[j], acts[i]
			}
		}
	}
	last := -1
	res := [][2]int{}
	for _, a := range acts {
		if a[0] > last {
			res = append(res, a)
			last = a[1]
		}
	}
	fmt.Println("Selected", res)
}
PYTHON
acts=[(1,4),(3,5),(0,6),(5,7),(8,9),(5,9)]
acts.sort(key=lambda x:x[1])
res=[]; last=-1
for s,e in acts:
    if s>last: res.append((s,e)); last=e
print('Selected',res)
SCALA2
object ActivitySelection extends App{
  val acts = Array((1,4),(3,5),(0,6),(5,7),(8,9),(5,9))
  val sorted = acts.sortBy(_._2)
  var last = -1; val res = scala.collection.mutable.ArrayBuffer[(Int,Int)]()
  for((s,e) <- sorted) if(s>last){ res += ((s,e)); last = e }
  println("Selected " + res)
}
SCALA3
@main def runActivity() =
  val acts = Array((1,4),(3,5),(0,6),(5,7),(8,9),(5,9))
  val sorted = acts.sortBy(_._2)
  var last = -1
  val res = scala.collection.mutable.ArrayBuffer.empty[(Int,Int)]
  for ((s,e) <- sorted) if s > last then res += ((s,e)); last = e
  println("Selected " + res)
TYPESCRIPT
const acts: [number,number][] = [[1,4],[3,5],[0,6],[5,7],[8,9],[5,9]]
acts.sort((a,b)=>a[1]-b[1])
const res: [number,number][] = []
let last=-1
for(const [s,e] of acts) if(s>last){ res.push([s,e]); last=e }
console.log('Selected', res)
export {}
